attempted_route: Followed round-1 recipe exactly: load all 3 .npy files, flatten each independently with arr.reshape(len(arr),-1), fit StandardScaler+Ridge(alpha=1.0) on train, transform X_test, predict, inverse-transform, reshape to (N_test, channels, time), save.
observation: ValueError at scaler_X.transform(X_test): X_test has 40000 features but StandardScaler expects 3308000; train_sub01 and test_sub01 have different per-sample feature dimensions after independent flatten.
rationale: train_sub01 and test_sub01 differ in shape beyond just N_samples: flattened train features=3308000/N_train vs test features=40000/N_test. StandardScaler fit on train cannot transform test with a different feature count. Root cause is dataset structure not matching a naive 2D reshape.
recommended_alternative: After loading, print shapes of all three arrays and inspect sub-dimensions. If test_sub01.shape[1:] != train_sub01.shape[1:], average or select matching time/channel axes before flattening (e.g. test_sub01[:, :train_sub01.shape[1], :train_sub01.shape[2]]) then apply reshape(len(arr),-1) to ensure feature counts match before fitting StandardScaler and Ridge.
